problem.statement: Partners, captains and field representatives need one WLT-owned payout lifecycle that binds every request to an actor-owned protected destination, enforces maker-checker separation, survives ambiguous provider outcomes, and exposes canonical scoped readback across all required surfaces.
problem.currentWorkaround: Payout requests and provider processing exist, but destination ownership is keyed only by an untyped partner identifier, field routes can collide with partner identifiers, captain and partner destination surfaces are missing, requests are not bound to a destination, and unknown provider outcomes do not have a payout-specific governed reconciliation action.

actors.role: Authenticated partner managing and using only its own payout destination and reading only its own payout requests
permittedActions: create payout against own active destination
forbiddenActions: read or mutate another actor destination
approve process complete or reconcile a payout

actors.role: Authenticated captain managing and using only a personal payout destination
permittedActions: create payout against own active destination
forbiddenActions: use a partner or field destination

actors.role: Authenticated field representative managing and using only a personal payout destination
permittedActions: create payout against own active destination
forbiddenActions: update a destination not owned by the authenticated field actor

actors.role: Authorized control-panel operator executing separated payout decisions and reconciliation
permittedActions: inquire and reconcile unknown provider result
forbiddenActions: approve and process the same payout
process and complete the same payout
manually fail an unknown result without inquiry

actors.role: Identity-aware proxy that resolves the authenticated actor server-side
forbiddenActions: trust actor identifiers from a mobile request body

actors.role: Sole financial truth owner for destination, holds, provider proof, ledger, payout audit and reconciliation
forbiddenActions: reuse idempotency key for different destination or amount

actions: enforce one active destination per actor

outcome.statement: Every payout request is bound to one active destination owned by the same typed actor, every operator transition is separated and idempotent, every provider result is proven or reconciled, and every financial effect is balanced, audited and surfaced canonically.
outcome.primaryMetric: destination-bound payout requests completed with canonical provider proof
outcome.baselineState: Provider-backed payout processing exists but destination ownership and multi-surface bindings are incomplete and unknown outcomes lack a payout-specific reconciliation lifecycle.
outcome.targetState: WLT owns one typed, encrypted, destination-bound, maker-checker, provider-proven and reconcilable payout lifecycle across partner, captain, field and control panel.
outcome.observationWindow: targeted static database contract backend frontend finance security runtime and independent review

criteria: Destination owner type is one of partner captain or field and owner identity always comes from an authenticated DSH self route.
Only masked destination values leave WLT; raw account IBAN and mobile-money values remain encrypted at rest.
A payout request references an active destination owned by the same actor type and actor id.
Idempotency hash includes actor destination amount and currency, and a conflicting replay is rejected.
Approval processor and completer are distinct authenticated operators.
Provider submission is single-claim and ambiguous outcomes remain held until governed inquiry resolves them.
Completion requires successful provider reference and posts one balanced ledger transaction atomically.
Failure or rejection releases held funds exactly once and never after successful provider proof.
Every destination and payout transition appends correlation-linked audit and notification outbox evidence.
Partner captain and field surfaces expose only their own destination and payout lifecycle; control panel exposes only legal actions.
requiredChecks: OpenAPI and generated binding drift check
targeted shared and surface TypeScript typecheck
provider process and inquiry runtime smoke

business: WLT exclusively owns destination payout wallet ledger provider proof audit and reconciliation truth.
DSH resolves actor identity and never accepts owner identity from a mobile body.
Every payout mutation carries service authentication correlation and idempotency.
Every visible success is read back from WLT.
Unknown provider results keep funds held until inquiry and reconciliation.
negative: No raw bank account IBAN or payout mobile value leaves WLT.
No destination is shared across actor types.
No request uses another actor destination.
No protected transition is performed by the same operator.
No payout is submitted to the provider twice.
No held amount is released or paid twice.
No payout is completed without provider proof.

owners.productManager: DSH and WLT Financial Product Management

execution.appetite: Close JRN-037 code data contract lifecycle and required surface gaps without moving financial truth out of WLT, enabling production providers, deploying production or self-approving protected evidence.
fixedConstraints: Repository mode is REMOTE_ONLY on sambassam.
WLT remains the sole financial truth owner.
No PR merge force push release production deployment or SaaS activation is authorized.
Independent product finance security QA release and risk approvals cannot be self-issued by engineering.
unknowns: Runtime migration and provider inquiry outcome
